reviews.review_description: What a phenomenal VR adventure. The Phoenix Gene is a celebration of imagination, letting you embody a phoenix and take flight, transitioning between worlds. I can’t say enough about the artistry and storytelling as it transports you into a realm of possibility. The flight mechanics are graceful and the world-building is rich and meaningful. I can’t wait to see what this team comes up with next!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much.

reviews.review_description: Great fantasy shooter so what u do is u are trying to help this girl Jackie that's getting shot by theese robots that u have to shoot with fireballs because you control a pheinox

reviews.review_title: Definite classic Star Fox gameplay vibes
reviews.review_description:
Some of my favorite parts of the game would be spoilers. So, I’ll just start by simply saying that I absolutely love This game!

Definite classic Star Fox gameplay vibes, except that you can now virtually control and guide your hero instantly with either hand as needed. 

There is much replay value for not only upgrades and achievements, but just for the pure adrenaline rush alone. 

It also has a very intriguing story that wonderfully manages to surprise and has some delightful twists I wasn’t expecting.

developer_response.body: Thank you non-exist-ent. Much appreciated. Glad you're liking it.

reviews.review_title: A fun arcade game with potential!
reviews.review_description:
I gave The Phoenix Gene three stars ☆☆☆ upon launch, but after revisiting the game almost two months later, I now give it 3.5 stars out of five. (Further details below)

To start with, the visuals are fun and colorful! The phoenix character model looks very cool and is highly detailed. It's also super cute and well-animated, and I never got tired of looking at it in my hand over hours of play time.

As a rail shooter, it's not afraid to go bold and try some really fun things with the camera (your perspective) - I won't spoil the details in this review. Despite the fun of some of the more adventurous (for VR) on-rails camera movement, I didn't experience a single moment of motion sickness! That makes this game very easy to recommend for both hardcore VR players with iron stomachs and casual players alike.

Some of the voice acting is pretty rough in its current state, which did reduce my emotional engagement, but it's clear they've put a lot of attention into the story they're trying to tell. If they improve some of the voice acting, that would go a long way to improving the experience of the story. I *did* get confused at certain moments in the story, but that's likely just a result of my ADHD and inability to focus on conversation/narrative while engaging in gameplay. Likely not an issue for most players.

Ultimately, gameplay is the most important thing here, and on that front, I can report that The Phoenix Gene is fun and doesn't overstay its welcome! This type of game is fairly novel for VR, and from what I played, there's enough variety of gameplay and environments to last the duration of the playtime (I estimate a full playthrough of the game would be roughly six hours, depending upon difficulty).

I was personally unable to finish the game in my initial review due to my inability to beat a boss in Chapter 4 (the game has eight chapters), so I couldn't progress further. I tried several times for over an hour and got nowhere, but when I returned to the patched version post-release, I managed to beat the boss in only a few tries! I advise using the laser pointer "aim assist" for this battle if you're struggling. Regardless of this, I still enjoyed the roughly four hours of playtime otherwise in my initial review! 

I should note that, as I was playtesting the game, I did get it for free - but I was *very* mindful to review the game as if I'd paid full price for it, and now that I've written it, I feel the final review reflects that.

UPDATE: playing the game a little over a month post-release, I feel there are some "telegraphing" issues for the upgrade/health system (I could never tell if/when I was low on health, and could find no instruction as to what the health indicators were). I also had some trouble following the story while engaging in gameplay, but I suspect this won't be an issue for most players. I enjoyed some of the adventurous moves of the on-rails camera (uncommon for VR), and if the experience shown in the trailers looks to be up your alley, give it a try!

developer_response.body: Your health is low when your character is almost black and flashing. Thank you for your updated review The_CinemaNaut. We have another update on it's way.

reviews.review_title: A Fresh Twist on VR Shooting
reviews.review_description:
The Phoenix Gene surprised me in the best way. It’s got a unique vibe that stands out from other on-rails VR shooters, both in how it plays and how it tells its story. The hand-switching mechat nic to control your dragon/phoenix is actually pretty cool once you get the hang of it, and adds a layer of immersion you don’t always see.

The gameplay leans into bullet-hell territory at times with the color-switching shield mechanic (blue absorbs blue, orange absorbs orange), which keeps you on your toes and gives it some strategic depth. It’s not the easiest game out there, but the challenge is fair—and thankfully, you don’t have to restart everything from scratch when you fail.

Visually, the game pops on Quest 3. The environments are colorful, the movement is smooth, and the dreamlike cutscenes sprinkled throughout the levels are an interesting touch. The story is there if you want to dive into it, but it doesn’t get in the way if you’re just in it for the action.

That said, there were a few moments early on where reaching gems or items felt awkward—mostly due to the hand tracking and positioning. It’s a small thing, but having an option to tweak the reach distance or make the hitboxes more forgiving would go a long way, especially for seated players.

If you’re into VR shooters with a twist and don’t mind a bit of a learning curve, this is worth checking out. Definitely one of the more memorable titles I’ve played recently.

developer_response.body: Thanks Csonis. Try one of our smaller room scale options from the lobby if you'd prefer a more relaxed seated experience.

reviews.review_description: The gameplay mechanics feels like no other. Its mind of nice that you have a skill tree to upgrade what you need for the ongoing levels so on and so forth but aiming at enemies you need to shoot feel a little weird as you can move the dragon from the left of your hand or to your right if you want to switch hands, sometimes you are required to do so and you need to avoid pillars and obstacles to prevent taking damage. One thing if i wish they were to fix is add some sort of red dot so i know what im aiming at cause some of the shooting mechanics felt hard at the start of the game. But sticking to the positive great game!

developer_response.body: Thanks Yoshi1673. A dot or reticule doesn't work in 3D space when you can see in stereo with two eyes. That only works in 2D on flat screens. We have provided a laser pointer, which you can turn on in the options but it's less distracting simply holding your arm up and aiming like a hand gun when you need accuracy.

reviews.review_description: This is a very fun rail shooter game, reminds me of the old temple run games you used to play on your phone, as not only do you have to shoot down enemies and avoid their attacks, you also need to avoid obstacles that get in your way, which is just a generally fun and addicting formula. It's also surprisingly physical, a good workout that doesn't get tiring too quickly. The environments are all really cool, the art style is great for VR, very pretty to look at for the most part. The realm toggle mechanic is a really unique way to make the gameplay more varied, and overall the multiversal time travel story is fun to engage with. My biggest issue with the game is the fact that you can press play without setting your room size, so I know that'll be really frustrating for people with smaller rooms who may not notice that setting is there when they get into the menu, so I think it should be set with a popup when you press play. I also feel the aim assist should be one that gets toggled on or off before the game starts, or at least highlighted in the tutorial because you will not know it's there unless you go searching for it. Overall, pretty solid game that just needs one or two important quality of life changes.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback 300bottles. We can look into those. We aimed to deliver the best version of the game to most people first then let them customize it. It's tricky as no-one can know what they really want until they've at least tried the game. We can ask the question first and they just might ruin the experience for themselves.

reviews.review_title: Most Unique Rail Shooter I've Played
reviews.review_description: This is by far the most unique rail shooter I've played. I am playing it during Early Access/ testing period. Without giving away the story, you can change colors to defend/attack against different enemies and to pick up collectibles. It is definitely more challenging than it seems at first. I love the difficulty and roomscale options. I'm only 2 levels in and I can't wait to get further along.

developer_response.body: Great, I hope you like the other levels... you're only just getting warmed up. But thank you for the review.

reviews.review_title: A decent rail shooter that shows promise
reviews.review_description:
First of all the story and lore surrounding the game is very well done and voice acted. While flat at times it is very appreciated that some thought and care was put into how you could consume the storyline.

This is a rail shooter where your hand can be the protagonist.

Pros: engaging storyline, voice acting and gameplay 

Cons: very minor things like I wish I could set.the default position of the player character more in line with where my hand actually is. When moving around it feels at times rubber bandy.

I look forward to continued updates to this one!

developer_response.body: Thanks DarkestHour111. The Phoenix moves towards your hand based on which grip buttons or joystick you're activating, and it all depends on where your controller or headset is. It's quite complex, but to keep it fluid and intuitive while also giving some realism this was the best solution. A tighter match to one hand was evaluated but that's really a different game, more like every other gun game on the market, so we were trying something different. Later in the game you do unlock something special that makes the speed of the character reaching your hand faster.

reviews.review_title: Controlling a dragon is a blast
reviews.review_description:
I've had a lot of fun testing The Phoenix Gene. The main control mechanic is really enjoyable. Controlling Firestorm instantly triggered my inner child.
 The range of difficulty and physical activity available is also impressive.  The physical dodging and reaching is enough to get a workout. I also tried seated in a spinning chair, and that was surprisingly engaging as well, since I was doing quite a bit of rotating at some points.  
 Catching the jumpers was my favorite level so far. The combination of laughing at the npcs I missed, and the exhilaration of falling was memorable.  
 I'd like to see some more flexibility of Firestorm's animations when trying to shoot in directions other than straight ahead.   The ability to unlock moves like barrel rolls etc. as well as time trial type levels without enemies could be fun. 
 Maybe a button combo with trigger to allow an automatic fire mode to reduce trigger spam since trigger hold is used by the flame.  
 Overall enjoyable game. Recommended.

developer_response.body: Thank you SpaceSweatYZ. We've been exploring the autofire option and so far we've only seen how it would ruin the game experience relative to other more unique mechanics we're already doing. But we're staying open to it and if we find a solution that works we'll add it.

reviews.review_description:
This game is truly one-of-a-kind both unique and intensely challenging! I love how you control the character’s movements with your hands, shooting your way through levels while strategically using abilities to progress. I’ve never played anything like it before!

That said, the gameplay is no joke; it’s extremely demanding, and after a session, my arm is exhausted from holding it up for so long lol. But that just adds to the thrill!

Highly recommended for anyone looking for something fresh and engaging. You won’t be disappointed!

developer_response.body: Thank you DeafyPro. If you want an easier session try our smaller room scale options in the lobby.

reviews.review_title: Extremly fun and engaging Arcade shooter
reviews.review_description: This game was an absolute blast to play. I'm only a couple hours in and can definitely see myself sinking more into it. IT has an extremely engaging story, gorgeous visuals, and some really fascinating mechanics. I do wish that it was more clear where you were aiming though, I had a decently hard time getting a feel for where i was going to be shooting for the first couple of levels.

developer_response.body: Thanks WinterRanger92. You can turn on the aim assist in the options if you need it, but the aiming works the same as shooting a gun. If you hold you imagine holding your arm up to aim down the top of a hand gun and pull the trigger, it'll hit what you're aiming at.

reviews.review_description:
This is one of those hidden gems that comes along from time to time and you just say WOW.
The graphics are so vibrant and colourful it looks stunning on Quest 3.

I love the mechanics of the game and controlling the dragon  is very unique, flipping from hand to hand and using the trigger button fires flames from the dragon.

Adventure story games are my absolute favourite and I am already loving this, I will fight to keep my dragon alive to save the day.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing GizmoDemi, we really appreciate your kind review. So glad you saw what we're aiming for here. We have updates on the way, so more to come. Keep playing!

reviews.review_description:
Well, first the story is well made, is has sense but sometimes hard to follow when the characters are speaking while you are shooting.

Secondly the graphics are clear and adapted to a quest 3 !

Third, the game is using the full dimensions, you will have to look above, under you and in every directions.

Lastly, it's not always adapted for small playspaces.

developer_response.body: Thank you IMisterlight. We do have four room scale options available to choose from in the lobby for smaller play spaces.

reviews.review_description:
Phoenix Gene is a fun and wonderful VR-based arcade style game with 8 different levels to complete and 7 individual powerups to earn (powerups are earned based on the number of “flame” points collected while playing.  The game can be played either sitting or standing, with various levels of difficulty to choose from. 

I am testing this game during the early access stage and I found Phoenix Gene to have fewer bugs and glitches versus various similar games at full release. I believe that the obvious take away is that the full release will exceed expectations for games in this same genre. 

Pros
Excellent Graphics
Visually and performance-based
Beautiful, bold, and colorful opening graphic
Excellent audio
Individually adjustable sound controls
Music, master volume, etc
In-game sound effects are very appropriate for the arcade-style play environment
Light and optimized program code
Game loads/reloads quickly
Nicely constructed and intuitive game menu
Choice of continuing/restarting previously played levels or beginning a new and separate game
The green play button action is a little confusing at first.
  You have to ensure that the current level is selected or you will start from the “slow” beginning scene again at level 1
The game immediately restarts the same level should you get killed or not meet the requirements for level succession
 Interesting concept/feature allowing the player to “see/pass through” various objects and obtain level-related points or benefits
The tutorial is very basic and shows the player:
How to press the controller trigger button to fire at targets
How to maneuver the dragon you control by moving/extending your arms
Additional movement/control information is provided throughout the game as you earn powerups

It took me some time to completely understand using the “A” or “X” buttons to change my dragon color to the appropriate incoming fire color and recognize the benefits
This becomes more manageable as you gain experience

developer_response.body: Thanks for the thorough outline Disneypoppy! Good to see all your thoughts on it. We're working on more updates and polish.

reviews.review_description:
One word: Legit Engaging! Sorry that's two but trust me I kept diving back in after dying more times than I care to count. 

Multi fire is a beast, and when you time it right mid-flight, it's just chaos in the best way. Played seated, but even then, between dodging drones and launching attacks non-stop, your arms and shoulders feel it by the end. If you are into fast paced challenges with layered progression, this one's worth locking into. 

Deducting 1 point for lack of a quick reference guide for controls. There are in-game info for controls but not a reference for a quick lookup.

developer_response.body: Thanks SigmaBlaze. Note taken... although I've always found those info-graphics a bit cheap. We could add one if we hear more people want it. Instead we have a tutorial that can be revisited and adjusts to include some new features as they are learned.

reviews.review_title: A very cool take on rail shooters!
reviews.review_description:
Disclaimer: i recieved this game for free to test and review.

Phoenix Gene is an interesting game where you control a phoenix (a fire bird), fighting against robots that try to stop the player and the phoenix itself. The game has a well-made story and is generally very stable, with no impactful bugs. However, there's one thing that pulls it down from 5 to 4 stars for me: the gameplay isn't very fast. In my opinion, it would be more fun (and exciting) if the game were a bit faster.

However, I do recommend this game. You don't see rail shooters often anymore, and it's great that there are still people who dare to make these kinds of games!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback Nickje2000. The game ramps up the speed over time. Many players are put off by it moving too fast too soon, and there's no easy way to adapt to what the player wants. So we start slow and straight, until the player gets used to how to play, then introduce more turns and faster speeds gradually, until eventually it can be quite intense. It's really cool in later levels! There's nothing like it.

reviews.review_description:
This is an interesting Rail-Shooter, if you like this genre. You basically fly on the back of a dragon. You can control it directly with either of your hands by "grabbing" it. That's how you navigate past/through the level architecture and avoid being shot by robot drones. You also alternate between fire and ice which becomes important at some point, because you have enemies that have to be attacked by either attack. Make sure you have space around you, you will need it if you want to reach certain items that might otherwise be too far away to reach. You will be moving more than you will expect. I'm kinda stuck at a boss atm and need to play more.

Update: Thanks @dev for the information, I'll definitely check it out again.

developer_response.body: Thank you Tryyton. To clarify for readers, the player doesn't fly on the back of the Phoenix... that would be vomit inducing and has various downsides that would make the game less compelling. But you do control the Phoenix in your hands in third person. We have added an easy mode in the last update, and we're expanding the difficulty options in our next update coming soon. Thanks again for playing, hopefully the current easier setting will get you further into the adventure.

reviews.review_description:
I have played this game during the testing period. It was definitely one of the most interesting games that I’ve tested so far. I am enjoying the story and the gameplay mechanics. Oh and this game has given me quite the workout. I am mid-way through the campaign as of writing this review and I am planning on completing it this weekend.

I will update the review if it becomes necessary.

developer_response.body: Great, glad you're enjoying it! Thanks Chuck.

reviews.review_description: The game mechanics are really fun...tutorial is great for explaining how it's all going to work.  The story grabs you and who doesn't want to fly as a dragon? Definitely worth adding to your library.

developer_response.body: Thank you GypsyyKris

reviews.review_description:
It was a fun game, don’t get me wrong. There are good levels, but if you can’t have a roomscale boundary it is difficult, even with the settings for it. Also no matter your settings the scale feels off.
The background was beautiful and so were the enemies, but the people seemed… clunky. The voices weren’t lined up well with the mouths. I know it is sometimes hard to do, but I feel like it is worth it. 

The game was really fun and challenging at points and the slipstream was amazing. I kept coming back to the game no matter what I thought. It was a great idea, well thought out, the lore was interesting, and I hope that the game can be improved. I didn’t finish the game, so I still plan to do that.

Edit for the developer: I did try the smaller world options. I tried all of them except the bigger world options. Even with this, in scenes where you are flying either up or down it is still very difficult.

developer_response.body: Thanks Disguy54. Try the smaller room scale options for smaller spaces or seated play. It's an option in the lobby, second from the left.

reviews.review_description: The Phoenix Gene is a nice rail game where you control a phoenix and help Jackie save her life. The game is really fun you control a phoenix and fire at robot and try to survive. The difficulties level is good enough.  The game ain't perfect, but I had a lot of fun so far. Hope the developer will continue to work on it and bring more stuff to it. I definitely recommend this game for peoples who love rail style games.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing Omegaprimal77. We do have updates planned, the first patch will be out very soon and addresses some difficulty issues we're hearing from our testers. We also have other optimizations and fixes coming. Glad you liked it and thank you so much for taking the time to review it.

reviews.review_description:
Played during Beta:
TL;DR 
Reccomended if you like bullet hells and moving around. Story could use some work. The Aim Assist option is just a laser pointer so don't be afraid to use it.

Pros:
- The game looks great, good fidelity and they make good use of the art style they've chosen.
- The gameplay was surprisingly good, with the difficulty ramping up giving you a LOT to keep track of. 
- Once more mechanics are added beyond didge and shoot, you really do get challenged by the levels and what you have to keep track of.
Con:
- However, the story feels like a very was ageneric "big corpa bad" deal and wasn't really told (writing-wise) in a way that piqued my interest.
Pro:
-  I DO appreciate that most story and dialogue was happening in game, so it did not force me to sit through long 2D cutscenes in a VR headset. 
Con:
- However some of the animations and Voice Acting during the few cutscenes are distractingly bad and I would have prefered even a stylized animatic style of stop motion animation.

Overall, a decent game with some work to be done in the storytelling department.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback Krayne.

reviews.review_title: Drives me mad, i love it
reviews.review_description:
I wouldn't have thought that another game would make me miss dinner. The game here captivates me far beyond what this one does.
It was a little confusing to follow the story at first, and yes, the game has a story.
At first I thought well, just action, can't be that hard, and then came the first difficult passage, I was desperate but despite failure, the game didn't let me go, I wanted to know what would happen next, when my wife threatened me that she wouldn't cook dinner if I played phoenix Gen, because then I wouldn't get to eat either way, I realized that the game deserved 5 stars, and maybe even a second part.
I am ready

For me the Phoenix Gene deserves the Tag addictive more then another at this time.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing WillieMcBride. Don’t miss dinner! :)

reviews.review_title: Nice movement game with okay campaign
reviews.review_description:
This is a fun little game that is 50% bullet hell and 50% beatsaber. Surprisingly, it leads to a rather nice combination. The visuals are okay, the sound is also good. The story is a bit too convoluted, even though it is on rails - there is too much going on given how short the game is, at least in my opinion. Its doesn't have a rougelike replayability, but the fact that you unlock new abilities as you go along the story gives you a reason to come back to a level once or twice. The level design itself is sometimes good, sometimes questionable - there are some moments that you simply cannot anticipate that will kill you and force a reset to checkpoint. However, shooting and movement mechanics themselves are okay, but I recommend playing on'seated' mode, even when standing. The game doesn't take your player space into account at all and will actively send you to ER if you aren't careful.

Note: The game has been received for free in a prerelease playtest.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing. We have added two more world scale options to help players choose a scale appropriate to their play space. We do lean into getting the player to move more though just as a fitness game or beat saber does.

reviews.review_title: A bit of fitness with a story
reviews.review_description: This game makes you move around so make sure you got some space. It's not fitness as you would think in other apps/games but more of a stretching and movement challenge as you play through a storyline so you get to game, get to experience a narrative, and get to do it without feeling too guilty about gaming.

developer_response.body: Yes it's a sneaky way to get a bit of a workout in while enjoying some gaming. Thanks for the review ArchiRobbie. Note that we have room scale options in the lobby, and we're adding more.

reviews.review_title: Crazy but fun at the same time
reviews.review_description: I love the way that a story and VR come together in this action packed game , I found the bosses to be "Crazy but fun at the same time " as you have to go in a few times to get used to the colour changing power ups that is percent to you when battling with the bad guys, over all a well made gaming experience that will get better and better over time .

developer_response.body: Thanks ScottishGamerVR. Much appreciated.

reviews.review_title: A fiery experience burning with action!
reviews.review_description:
As someone who loves seeing innovation in the VR space it was amazing to see a new way to move a character through a world. 
The action in this game is multi-layers and takes a lot of practice to get used to, and is fun to learn.
The fact theres also a full story about the lore of this universe is also a very nice part of the game.
10/10 highly recommended

developer_response.body: Much appreciated Redsparx. Thank you for noticing!

reviews.review_description: The Phoenix Gene is a fun game where you controll a dragon in a rail system, almost like Subway Surfers type movement. You must avoid enemies and shoot them down. This game is pretty fun and features a slightly deep story and lore to it.

developer_response.body: Thanks Flexfinity. I see some resemblance to Subway Surfers but it's a stretch. :)

reviews.review_title: Flying and Firing Through the Slipstream
reviews.review_description: Really cool concept with this one. Basic idea is your character automatically moves through the stage (while you move around to dodge and collect things) and enemies pop up that you shoot at. But they added an interesting mechanic of switching realities so you can determine which objects can hurt you, and which pass right through. Gameplay is simple yet challenging, and the upgrades you receive as you progress enable you to go back to previous levels and get better scores. Don't be frustrated if you feel like you can't do everything from the very first level ;) Lastly, a lot of effort was put in to the story - I was quite impressed!

developer_response.body: Thank you. We just added easy mode in our latest update and we’ll be putting more work into expanding features like that.

reviews.review_description: The Phoenix Gene is a great artistic game about a woman named Jackie who befriends a Phoenix/Dragon where we slowly start to find out about both of their backgrounds and reasons for helping each other (Very simplified as to not spoiler you). The story is fantastic and the graphics are great and uniquely artistic. The controls are simple, but definitely not easy! The game takes about 6-7 hours and is quite a workout! Can only recommend for people who like on-the-rail shooters!

developer_response.body: Thanks KindGentlemanThatisntKind

reviews.review_description:
I’m always on the lookout for new VR games that push the boundaries, and The Phoenix Gene absolutely blew me away. From the moment I slipped on my headset, it was clear this wasn’t just another shooter. The total immersion is wild your arms, your torso, your whole body gets pulled into the action as you control Firestorm, this massive, powerful phoenix, and Jackie, the enhanced human protagonist. The movement feels natural and keeps you on your toes; you really do have to fly, dodge, and twist to survive.

The “realm toggle” mechanic is a standout. Absorbing enemy bullets and switching realities mid-fight is both strategic and thrilling. It’s not just about shooting everything in sight; it’s about timing those toggles and reading the battlefield. I died a lot at first, but once I got the hang of it, the satisfaction of weaving through a storm of bullets with just the right realm switch is hard to describe.

Combat is fast and intense. Enemies attack from all directions: mutant beasts, scavenging raiders, and sleek killer drones. You need to stay alert and keep moving.

If you enjoy action-packed VR with real physical movement and a unique twist on classic shooter mechanics, The Phoenix Gene is a must-try. It's challenging but fair, visually stunning, and truly fresh in a crowded genre.

developer_response.body: Wonderful, really glad you are enjoying it Fearian!

reviews.review_description: With probably the most entertaining VR game storyline I have seen in at least a few years, I would highly recommend this game to anyone who loves a bit of lure! The gameplay felt very smooth and polished, better than some games I have played which are much ‘more’ developed and expensive! Great value for money.

developer_response.body: Thanks Neoliths, our writer will be pleased :D

reviews.review_description: The graphics look amazing on Quest 3—very vibrant and detailed. Controlling the phoenix with body movement is something different and really well done, super immersive. It's an active experience that pulls you into the action and gets you moving, which makes it a lot of fun. The combat is intense, with great visual effects and a real sense of speed. Highly recommended if you're looking for something original, dynamic, and polished in VR.

developer_response.body: Thank you MrPaks. We appreciate the kind words.

reviews.review_description: What a great game, really colorful and visually brilliant.  Just love controlling the dragon, this has an engaging story line I am looking forward to following as the game progresses.  It is not the easiest though and thankfully I have risen from the ashes to continue forward and not having to start from the beginning each time.  Early days so far but really enjoying it.

developer_response.body: Thank you Magic Merlin, we hear your concerns over the difficulty and there's an update coming soon that hopefully addresses it. Thank you so much for playing.

reviews.review_description: This is one of my favorite on rails shooters. Instantly playing the game I got vibes from panzer dragoon. The control scheme is unique you are able to switch the dragon between hands like you are holding it. There is also elements of bullet hell games and an interesting mechanic where you switch different colors to match enemy shots. If you are blue you will absorb blue shots and heal. If you are orange you will absorb those projectiles. This keeps the game challenging and interesting. The story follows Jackie and I won't say too much to avoid spoilers but it is a nice complement to the action.  The graphics look good on quest 3. Would recommend to anyone looking for a fun on rails shooter.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review Sandwich. We're really glad you're liking it.

reviews.review_description: The Phoenix Gene game is out of this world the game itself is so fun because you can use your hands as the remotes .the graphics are superb . You get your blood pumping while playing because you get your body moving in this action packed game that reminds me of something out of one of the classic sci-fi novels but it comes to life on the vr .you go from wastelands to the futureall all while battling mutants scavengers and drones . The weapons are just right this game is really fun

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review Skyvuc, it sounds like you really get it... this is a bold new  approach to control in VR and not everyone gets it immediately. Thank you for sticking with it.

reviews.review_description: This game is rather interesting as it kind of gives the vibe of being a kid and playing with a fire breathing phoenix in your hand which is a good concept but in practice had me realizing I'm not exactly the target audience but that's ok because I feel like this game will be a hit for those in that target audience, the tutorial is thorough and the gameplay is easy enough not to frustrate anyone but offers enough challenge to not make the game too boring,  overall a decent game.

developer_response.body: Thank you GamingGuitarist. Much appreciated.

reviews.review_description: Good game with good graphics. I know not all people look for good graphics in games, but in this one they are good indeed, I also liked the fire effects, they were made really well. The level design I do like, it just wasn't quite convenient, that you can't really play seated, because you need to move a lot to collect everything and dodge the obstacles. Also, it seems like the stationary play space isn't enough for it either, you need to draw your own. I like the variety of different levels and enemies, but sometimes I find it hard to kill them all. Maybe it's just my skill issue. Overall a good game, since it's a stationary experience, it's gonna be a good one for new players, and those, who want to just try VR out for the first time.

developer_response.body: Thank you W0Iker. We've added an easy mode and more room scale options recently, and there's more to come. You should find that the smallest room scale combined with the snap turn will make playing seated work well. But the game does play at its best with a swivel chair with room to stand and move about.

reviews.review_description: The controls are very unique, passing the dragon from hand to hand feels weird at first, but then it becomes natural.  Make sure you have enough room in your play space, mine was a little small and I had issues grabbing all the flames. Everything else was amazing, it's a very pretty game. The levels are interesting and changing through the realities is an creative mechanic. I can't wait to play more and see more of the story. This is a great on rails shooter.

developer_response.body: Thanks Slim1337. Try the smaller room scale options in the lobby to make sure you don't have to reach too far in smaller spaces.

reviews.review_title: On rails shooter with good story telling
reviews.review_description:
Its an on rails shooter where you control your little phoenix to attack, collect and dodge around while taking in the story unfolding through unique levels.
The game plays around a mechanic where you have 2 forms each with alternating obstacles as well as making yourself immune to different projectiles. This makes it feel more engaging than just dodging around though it can get a bit overwhelming when a mix of projectiles are flying towards you. Visually the game is great and aome levels feature some fun routes to fly through that may or may not be easy to stomach for the motion sickness crowd, but i appreciate it. In some parts the pacing felt bit slow where i was basically just standing there and doing nothing, listening to story, but thats not too often. I wont go into the story in my review, but i can tell its interesting enough to jump in if you already have atleast some interest in the gameplay

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing gWinnieVR. We put a lot of attention into ensuring we aren’t doing the things that typically cause motion sickness. But we do try the challenge the player with unique directions of travel through the space for short periods. They should not cause sickness though.

reviews.review_description:
You play as the R2 gene carrier, Jackie. Your weapon is Firestorm, a phoenix you manipulate through obstacles and use like a firearm! The longer you play, the more abilities you gain automatically. This game takes you through a uniquely engaging storyline with ever-changing, enriched atmospheric colors. Being left-handed, I enjoy that you switch the phoenix between both hands, so I was able to use my left hand for the majority of the time. 
This is more of a standing game, and that's how I recommend playing. However, there is an option for using a swivel chair. I also used the subtitle option to catch all the dialogue and gameplay instructions.
There are checkpoints within the levels (who doesn't love that?), and the controls are easy to manipulate.
Overall, this is a fun on-rails shooter game, that takes you on an interesting adventure to discover who you really are and where you came from.

reviews.review_title: On rails shooter with a workout
reviews.review_description:
You control a dragon that shoots fire and can jump from left to right hand with the push button .
 Dodging and shooting similar to other games. But with a story and reaching to collect items.
You also have a button to toggle 2 different coloured dragons.  Giving you the ability to absorb same colour shots and change some backgrounds to fly or shoot through. 
Which is valuable because there are way to many shots to dodge as it gets more challenging.
Looks good and plays well. I’m enjoying my time playing and working towards upgrades. 
I recommend this game to anyone that enjoys on rails shooters

developer_response.body: Thank you Fleshthief

reviews.review_description:
Phoenix Gene is an On-Rail Shooter in the likes of Games like the Panzer Dragoon Series, where you control a Dragon by grabbing it with your Controller. 
It also features the Ability to switch to two different Colours, making you absorb same coloured Enemy Shots and making Objects in the Levels dissapear/reappear, clearly inspired by Ikaruga. 
The Graphics are well done for an Indie Game, and the Campaign has an OK lenght, but i would say, the spike in Difficulty is too hard on medium atm. But good that there's an Easy Mode now.
I like that there is a Story to this Game, although i have to admit it mostly doesnt make Sense and is just weird at times. Still fun though.
The one Thing that breaks this Game a tiny bit for me, is that the whole Speed of the Dragon flying feels just so absurdly slow most of the Time. But then also the Movement of the Dragon isnt responsive enough, as it has too much delay to it. 
With a little more work to it, some future Updates could make this a real Gem. As of this Moment it isnt without its little flaws, but already quite fun.

developer_response.body: Thanks TAKEyaDOWN. The speed moving along the path is a tricky topic, we had to be very careful not to go too fast too soon before the player has gotten used to it. But it really picks up speed later on.

reviews.review_description:
Ever since I played Rush of Blood on the PSVR1 I've been a fan of rail shooters, it's a category that would only ever be improved when played in VR and The Phoenix Gene is another good title in the genre.
Phoenix Gene also isn't about just sitting back and shooting things, there's quite a lot of movement involved, which can be adjusted if you play standing or seated, so there's some accessibility options if you feel like moving around or have a smaller play space.
The levels are really bright and colourful with some good variation on both environments and what you have to do.
What I appreciate about this game is it's actually trying to tell a story along the way and not just going the easy route that expects the fun of shooting things to be enough, there's a lot of effort put into the story, the set pieces and boss fights and it really shows.
I do have a few issues or things I would probably change, there's a lot of rotating you have to do while traversing through levels so I would love an automatic rotation so I can just focus on moving up, down, left and right.
It's kind of difficult to determine how injured you become when being hit by enemy projectiles or colliding with the environment, your dragon gets blacker like it's burnt, but it's tricky to be able to tell quite how close you are to failing and having to replay from the last checkpoint, so I would like a health bar somewhere.
You unlock a few improvements along the way, such as more health, I would really like a skill tree and maybe a new game plus or something like that to add some replay-ability.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review AllUAreIsHistory. We do have a few replay-ability features, but we're adding an unlockable guide to those in the next update coming soon. You're not the first person to request an auto turn feature but we won't be adding that as it would cause nausea. It's important that turning the world around you is only done by the player. It's what causes travel sickness in the real world (when you're not in control of the vehicle) and we've done everything we can to eliminate the chance of that in our game. The health display in many other VR games is a glance down at something on your wrist. For us it's a glance down at your player character, but because that character can be in lots of possible positions I can see how it's not quite as intuitive. We do flash the character to warn you they're close to death. A more obvious fix is probably immersion breaking. But if enough people are bothered by it maybe we can add something more as an option. Regardless, thank you for the feedback and we're glad you've been enjoying the game.

reviews.review_title: Fun on rails dragon flight shooter
reviews.review_description:
Good controls and fun mechanics like changing colors to match incoming attacks and defend npc levels.
Nice graphics with vibrant colors and interesting level designs.
Can give a nice workout in standing mode when you need to dodge obstacles.
Developers are really responsive to user feedback with some great updates.

developer_response.body: Thank you Mindjive. Much appreciated.

reviews.review_description: Awesome game and consept. I would recommend the aim assist be renamed to retical and reworked to be better than the default controller guidelines.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback XristoferLee, and we appreciate the review.

reviews.review_title: Fly the Phoenix with your hands
reviews.review_description:
great unique way to play the game you have to fly the phoenix  a bird controlling with you hands to fly it from left to right hand and back to fly dodge and move around the unique worlds
so prepared to have some extra playspace because if you go left or right in your space too far you will hit thinks you really dont want todo also if you are sitting !
and this is also one of the games where you have to finish the level completely before you have have your powerups your founded around the city's for the next level it do not add more power on the level you played (if you die on the level) and add it to play the level again with more power next time!
also because there is only one level of difficulty now, lets hope there will be added easier and harder levels to progress 
i have been many times repeating the same world's again and again
but a amazing concept that i not really have seen to play a VR game by controller where the Phoenix have to fly around obstacles
and there is no global scoreboard system on this game like some games have to see where you are on the list with other players/friends like some great single user player games have added !

developer_response.body: Thanks McCoreBe. We are working on smoothing the difficulty and adding other difficulty settings. In the mean time check out the Activity options in the lobby as that will offer a smaller world scale making you less likely to hit things in smaller rooms. More of those options coming soon too.

reviews.review_description:
Phoenix Gene a tough but unique take on a on- the-rail shooter where you guide Jackie and her Dragon across a series of levels.

The game takes inspiration from both Panzer Dragoon and Ikaruga with a difficult to almost match them too!
(It has an Ikaruga style switching mechanic where you can absorb shots of a particular colour for those wondering!)

Guiding your Dragon around like your holding a fictional dragon in your hand brought a child like glee in me (pretending to fly toy helicopters)

There's a 20+min voiced book excerpt within the game (and 3 more comming soon) for those invested enough in the story by for others there is still an intense shoot em up here.

The only downside is perhaps the longevity/replayability of the title (its still a decent length Id say 6-8 hrs or more if you fail often)
But I still had a blast playing through the game

developer_response.body: Thanks Burntbreadman. We have a few things already built into the game that should offer more replay-ability for completionists... but we have plans to explain them further and unlock the path to understanding them after a certain amount of play time... in the next update coming very soon.

reviews.review_description:
This was a fun and exciting on rails shooter.  The game had some great controls passing your phoenix from hand to hand  felt great.  Reminded me of playing as a kid.  You could change colors to absorb different attacks.  
    The graphics were good and very colorful.  While the levels had a nice mix and flow to keep things interesting.  I loved the story progressing as you follow Jackie on her journey.  
    They had a quick audiobook you could listen to if you really like to get more into the story.  With 3 more coming soon.  
   The difficulty was just right to keep thing intense.  While some of the objectives took longer to get used to than others.  I did struggle a little noticing how close I was to death when I got real low but did notice the flash warning for last hit and done so that helped.

developer_response.body: Thank you TbcVRGaming. Really glad you're liking it.

reviews.review_title: Great colourful game, with cool graphics
reviews.review_description:
The game has great settings and achievements and upgrade system.
The graphics are very good ,very colourful and gameplay is great to but |think the phoenix needs speeding up on movement, it's a bit to slow to dodge incoming shoots and reaching for some items is difficult !
The Phoenix does look amazing and the story behind it, as you are Jackie.
would recommend it , it's a keeper.

I'm really enjoying it the playability is excellent to progress through the levels.

As for any glitches, not really found any that make the game underwhelming.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review Greekspartan121. We're already working on updates and fixes, and should be sending the first patch out soon. Note that when you unlock a certain playable [spoiler] you'll be able to move faster. :)

reviews.review_description: Nice combination of rail shooting and hand-flying shooting. The phoenix can be controlled by just looking eye sight freely or hand grapping for quick movement. At first the stage boss is not so easy to pass. But there is upgrade system by collecting points and increasing my ability.

developer_response.body: Glad you liked it Carpedian. Thank you for the review.

reviews.review_description:
This is a great game, but it's still not polished enough.
In this game, your character rides a phoenix, and you fight with these robots and enemies. This wasn't really my style of game, but I know that other people like this type of game. There is also this comic book about the story, but i didn't really get into it, but if you like the story, you can read their books.

In some parts of the game, you can't reach some of the stuff you can collect while you play the game and the difficulty is really hard but I heard that there is an easy mode now so you don't have to worry about challenging gameplay.

developer_response.body: Thanks ghosp, more polish is on it's way. We issued an update today, in fact including difficulty settings and adjustments to the distance of early collectables and boss battles. We'll keep working on it to make it the best it can be. Thanks for the feedback.

reviews.review_description:
This is really nice looking game with awesome rail shooting where world keeps changing and you need to navigate trough them + shoot your way with horde of enemies.

Really good work out as you need to swap hands order to dodge enemies fire. Environments are all different then any other like no same aspect in these levels. You also collect diamonds and change your dragon color from hot to cold!

I definitely recommend this game to rail shooter fans and those who like dragons who shoot fire and ice!

developer_response.body: Great, glad you liked it GMspur! We're also pleased you saw the value in it as a workout. We have two room scale settings, that can be selected from the lobby for different levels of activity. From what we're hearing it may be worth adding more room scale options. Keep an eye on our updates for that. We have a roadmap of coming changes on our website. Thanks again for playing.

reviews.review_description: The graphics are super bright, colorful and pretty. It looks amazing on the Quest 3! Flying the dragon is so fun and takes a different approach to shootem ups and on rails shooter. Playing standing up is the preferred way. You will be moving your entire body and using both hands to control the dragon. It takes a little time to adjust at first, but once you find your dragon legs, it becomes second nature. I had some Ikaruga vibes playing it.

developer_response.body: Yes, Ikaruga was a big inspiration for the game. Along with Starfox, and Pistol Whip.

reviews.review_description: This game evokes a sense of comraderie I hold dear. The story is way more enjoyable than I expected, you can also experience it similarly to an audiobook in the main menu. The gameplay seems pretty linear at first but they evolve it and flip it on its head with boss fights or by shifting the angles and scenery. It manages to capture a sense of wonder with all the experiences ou go through. If you're looking for a story game with an engaging threat in every level, an entertaining story and and stunning level design or just like pheonixes then this is the game for you.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much.

reviews.review_description:
The phoenix game has amazing graphics, which does not lag my Quest 3 headset. The story is fully voiced and very well written, keeping me coming back since I want to complete it.

The gameplay is fun, steering your phoenix with your controller to dodge, shoot, and blast your way to the ending. Switching between colors changes the world, making certain color bullets miss you and certain obstacles appear or disappear , which adds a cool twist on an already cool game!

Overall, certainly worth a play!

developer_response.body: Thank you Pipkinn1.

reviews.review_description: The Phoenix Gene is a great on-rail game where you control Firestorm, the awesome phoenix, and Jackie as you uncover the game's exciting story. It's super fun to blast enemy robots and dodge their shots so you don't die. I would recommend this game to anyone who loves on-rail shooters definitely worth checking out!

developer_response.body: Thank you Warmarck

reviews.review_description:
This is such a cool game for all ages. I use a Quest 3 and the graphics are very colorful and flying the phoenix is amazing. For this game you can stand or sit. I suggest if you sit make sure that it is a swivel chair, you will be swirling around. I will be playing this one a lot and I know my grandchildren will love playing this game.

You are navigating the phoenix to help save Jackie and the future. You start in all the destruction and go through different worlds throughout the game. You fly the phoenix with your controllers switching hands to move the phoenix in different ways to dodge all the enemies that are after you while you are trying to kill the bosses.

As you are flying you collect diamond flames and the color of the diamond flames will change the Phoenix from Fire to Ice. You can fire flames from the phoenix as you go along in the game. There are all sorts of different things you will be able to do in the game that you will learn as you go along. The game is very good at showing you how to use your controllers for different actions in the game.

You get really engaged in the game especially during the action times which makes for a good workout.  The graphics and storyline make it very easy to get fully immersed in the game. You will be wanting to pick your headset up over and over to keep playing this game.

developer_response.body: Thanks KRGoldy, and I appreciate you noticing the effort we put in to pace out the explanation of the gameplay mechanics. Glad you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_description:
Product received for free for testing.  

I think if you enjoy rail shooters this one's a definite must-try.  The art style is cool.  The sound design is decent.  It generally has a good difficulty curve and the levels are interesting enough to come back to and try for a better score.

Now for what brings this down a star for me:  The first issue that can be fixed is that the dragon will jump between my hands without me asking and it has ruined runs on levels.  If that were the only issue, I'd rate it 5-stars (since it will likely be fixed), but I also really didn't care for the story going on while I'm trying to beat the level.  I really barely had an idea of what was going on despite replaying the levels over and over.  Part of this could be how my brain works and how I forget names, but it really deterred from the experience for me having to multitask playing and listening to story.  (Even in calm moments I'm worried something will show up.)

Another potentially fixable thing is a random spike in difficulty on I believe the 2nd or 3rd level.  There's a point where everything is just coming at you and you have to shoot everything and I feel like it's intended for a future powerup you get, but I don't think I had that ability unlocked for that level when I went back to it.  I can't tell if it's intended or not, but I feel like it is, and it was very unenjoyable.

I also didn't understand how to set up seated mode until multiple levels in, so I was sitting in standing mode unable to do much of anything and confused.  

Another small nitpick is how the unlocks are linear and don't really excite me to play the game more when I get them.  I have the extra incentive from testing to do levels over and over and I still haven't managed maxing out.  (I'm like 80-95% on all of them.)  If I bought the game, I don't know how much longevity I'd get to justify the price and I feel like I would be disappointed, but not enough to rate this poorly because it's still a decent game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback ArroraMew. We can't reproduce the problem you're having with the character jumping from one hand to the other so I have to assume it's something unique to your setup or equipment. We are looking into an option to increase the dead-zone on the grip button in case that solves it for one other user with a sticky button. We're sending out an update very soon that adjusts the first boss difficulty. We've also renamed the Activity button in the lobby so players know it's related to scale. Hopefully the update will address a number of your concerns. Thank you for playing. We appreciate your feedback.

reviews.review_description:
An on-the-rails shooter that has you moving around a bit, shooting stuff and shifting phases. When it starts out you need a bit of room to reach around and dodge. However, they have added some additional scale settings, so playing in a chair like I usually do isn’t as bad, once you can change the settings after the tutorial. 

The game has a lot of story dialog to it as you fly along the specific path trying to help the one that has the phoenix gene. It plays on scale in some odd ways, where everything is normal scaled then you are downscaled so everything is bigger, then the person you are saving is back to normal but some things are still huge. Don’t think about it much, just dodge, shoot stuff, collect the flames and some other items along the way is what it really comes down to. 

The difficulty can range from boring dragged out dialog parts to suddenly being overwhelmed with changing colors to absorb bullets that are practically impossible to dodge. There is a linear upgrade system that will help you survive longer, like being able to withstand more damage and eventually you get the ability to extend your reach so you can get those hard to reach flames. It’s an interesting game, but once you realize it is just the same thing over and over till you are good enough to beat it, it gets kind of boring. I’d say it is better than some of those other rail experiences that claim to be exercise games and you punch the balls instead of shooting them though.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing Satconcook. We're expanding our difficulty settings in the next update to offer a challenge to more experienced players as well as an easy mode.

reviews.review_description: In this game you are riding on a Phoenix that you have to move around on rails as the level moves on by and you sit the enemies.  You get a good stretching exercise with this one.  It does have an interesting story to follow along to.  Some levels are a bit tedious and in wish checkpoints were a little more often but at least it has difficulty options.   Graphics are nice and bright and it felt comfortable moving through the worlds.  This is a nice few hours distraction.

developer_response.body: Thanks Hollywoodhammer! Glad you're liking it.

reviews.review_description:
First what I like about this game,
The art style is pleasing 
I don’t think there are many games in this genre 
The voice acting

What I don’t like,
Personally,story didn’t interest me a lot
Shooting alignment was disorienting
Some animations felt lifeless

Other things to note, 
Make sure you have a lot of room to move your arm around in
This is an on the rails shooter, so it could easily make you motion sick.
Checkpoints aren’t frequent so you might get frustrated (I did)

Overall, if this game interests you, I would wait for a sale and then get it.

(side note, I’m a playtester and got this game for free but it didn’t affect my review)

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback 01arcanum. If you'd be interested in expanding on your experience on our Discord we'd love to hear from you. We did everything we could to avoid doing most of the things that can cause motion sickness in VR. Moving forward without walking/running yourself is one of the things that can cause a sense of nausea in users early in their experience with VR but it's also something we quickly adapt to, unlike some of the other causes. It's also something that vast quantities of other VR experiences already do so it's reasonable to assume we're not the first experience for most users. More than most games doing this, we do our best to ease the player into it starting very slow, and working our way up to a more intense experience once they're used to it. But I would imagine everyone experiences that adjustment at different rates so I'm keen to hear your experience. We have had other reviewers say that it starts too slow, and they want it to build up faster. With limited resources it's hard to cater to both. But maybe we can do more in the future.

reviews.review_description: This game is an amazing story driven shooter. Incredible boss challenges and story line keep you fighting through, even if you fail you will find yourself pushing yourself to go further, faster, harder. Amazing graphics and smooth game play are a definite, can be played seated or standing. This is the title you want if you want a good story and workout combined.

developer_response.body: Nice! Thank you Oz81

reviews.review_title: Good Story, But bring a Swivel Chair.
reviews.review_description: The game is on rails. You are basically piloted through a story which is good enough that it makes you want to keep playing to see what happens next. The environments are full of things to shoot at as you pilot a dragon that can switch from Fire to Ice based on what needs to be destroyed. This is a true bullet storm type game as there is so much to take out at once it can be overbearing. The problem I have with this game is that at certain point there is almost too much going on and you miss certain parts of the scene. I guess this ads to replay value though as you are able to go back and play each level at different difficulties and search for missed collectables as well. The game has some pretty cool boss battles which feels epic and like you really are fighting giant creatures which was a nice touch. The only other thing that kinda bothered me was the li sync was way off with the characters  talking when I played this. Very noticeable but not a killer. The price of this game might be a bit high in my opinion at the current 24.99 but it does offer a great experience to anyone who is into games like YUKI and any type of bullet storm games. I would also highly recommend playing this game in a chair that swivels as it it makes seeing scenes and firing at all the enemies easier.

developer_response.body: Yes I would recommend a swivel chair for the best experience and for longer sessions. The game would have been far less interesting if we'd catered only to fixed chairs, but we can be played on the sofa with snap turn, and that works well... just like most other first-person shooters in VR. We are working on refinements to the difficulty and an update is coming very soon. Lip sync is a battle with the tools, tech and resources of an Indie Studio... I'm an animator and it tortures me not to be able to deliver perfect lip-sync. We'll get there though (some fixes in the next update). Thanks for playing TruGamer4Realz, I'm glad you've been enjoying it.

reviews.review_description:
Full disclosure: I was provided a copy of this game for free to playtest and review. 

In The Phoenix Gene, players are the young Jackie, a carrier of the Phoenix Gene (a genetic roadmap to immortality). She is guided by Firestorm, a talking Phoenix (that looks a lot like a dragon) who guides her through many realities in search of a way to stop the evil Life Rite Corporation from exploiting this discovery to malicious ends. Players control Firestorm on this rail shooter through various missions, with upgrades being unlocked over the course of the story, as well as additional bonuses earned by collecting flames and other items scattered throughout the levels.

Pros: 

- The graphics are decent in places (for an indie game). 
- Gameplay is challenging and rewards replaying levels with upgrades.
- Some of the upgrades are thematic, but underutilized (like the one in level 2). 
- The level design has some clever reality-bending set pieces (level 3 is a fun example). 
- Controls make some use of VR (controlling Firestorm). 

Cons:

- The perspectives of some locations seem unusually out of ratio and it is unclear if the player shrinks which is why we are so small compared to everything else around. 
- The game has some accessibility issues and only works seated if you’re able to use a swiveling chair to see behind you (enemies can fire at you there). 
- Some items will be out of reach for players initially and require upgrades in order to reach them, it might be a good idea to be aware of that in advance. 
- There is a minor tutorial bug where the first part won’t if you reselect the tutorial (this may have been resolved). 
- Standard enemy types are not that varied and attack patterns didn’t seem to change that often. Difficulty came from several “bullet hell” areas (that could be difficult based on your perspective/ability to view threats 360 degrees around Firestorm). 

Subjective: 

- I found that the Phoenix/Dragon Firestorm speaks far too often and it is largely exposition, explaining the story to the player rather than showing it by other means (which is a shame as the game demonstrates in other areas that it can tell the story by showing rather than telling).
- The story reads like a YA sci-fi fantasy novella, this may or may not be to your liking. I personally found the “chosen one” story to be generic and the dialogue wooden with far too much exposition.
- The game has a steep learning curve in places and also will reset areas if certain objectives aren’t met, this could alienate newer gamers from turning away after 1-2 levels. 
- Menus and control UI could be more futuristic/thematic and interactive versus using standard pointer guides with controllers. 
- Wish there was a better system for understanding how much damage Firestorm could sustain (I like the idea of visual cues on the Phoenix itself versus an screen UI, but maybe audio cues could also indicate low health).
- Wish that Firestorm could turn with player’s movement instead of facing forward (looks odd to see fireballs coming from back of head).
- The audiobook extra was different, I think that it might work better as a radio drama and/or a visual graphic novel (in 3D, just look at the comics from Lies Beneath). 
- It is a very active game (the developer notes this when the game loads, they mean it). 

Summary:

Growing up, I was a big fan of the Panzer Dragoon Saga from SEGA, so when I heard about this game and its control scheme I saw some parallels between the two. But then Firestorm spoke… and spoke some more, and on and on it went, explaining backstory after backstory, and I found that this really took me out of the game I was playing. But some clever level design kept bringing me back to explore new levels and areas. The 3rd level in particular (which had less dialogue than others) showcased the most potential.

The story and voiceovers were not my personal taste, but the gameplay (in parts) revealed glimpses of a very imaginative and immersive experience that I did enjoy. Several of the upgrades were not utilized as well as they could have been and I feel should have been unlocked from the beginning. And the upgrade that interacts with the environment was a clever idea that sadly gets let down by the level designs themselves. I know this developer is passionate about this project and it shows (they’re also open to feedback which is appreciated) in the regular updates it is receiving. 

For gamers wishing to support an indie developer with potential, I recommend this game with a solid 3-star rating. In time, I hope to see it rise from the ashes of reinvention and into a full 4 or 5-star game that I know it can be. Looking forward to future projects from this developer. 

A small addendum:

This developer should try contacting a SEGA rep (since the company has made some VR games) to see if they’ll collaborate on a VR reimagining of Panzer Dragoon. Show this game as proof of concept. There are a lot of nostalgia hungry VR gamers (and new gamers alike) that would like to see that project become a reality.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review BoardGameNick. As you say, we are actively making improvements and responding to player feedback. We have an update coming out soon that should address a number of concerns including more room scale options. We already cater to the needs of players in a fixed seat with our turn options and smaller room scale feature (currently in the Activity section of the lobby, but we're renaming that to make it more obvious). The update should also improve the difficulty curve. You mention the perspective of some locations... I think you mean the scale of the player character relative to the world around them, and this is really down to what plays well, how far the average player can move and how large their play space is. I've seen requests that sound good on paper but just aren't fun or practical in the real world. So we catered to what actually works, keeps players safe, not feeling sick and having fun. Even if it wasn't completely realistic. But that's games for you. Glad you liked it Nick. I hope you'll come back for more when the update lands very soon.

Update: we just released an update that addresses some of your concerns, including collectible reach distances, turning Firestorm during Boss battles and an easy difficulty setting. It’s an ongoing process. Please consider joining our Discord to let us know about any other concerns you feel we can improve upon.

reviews.review_description:
Try as long as possible to keep the Phoenix 🐦‍🔥 alive. Kill those drones and gather gems to get your health back up.

In the beginning it was not that easy (for me 😏) to indicate the direction of the phoenix (to collect the gems) and firing (to destroy the drones) at the same time. I had to try it over and over again…


Graphics are fantastic. 
The tutorial is very clear. Controls aren’t complicated.

When you like a game with a story, you definitely have to try Phoenix Gene.

developer_response.body: Great, thank you Powergirl.be

reviews.review_description:
I really would like to give this tittle more then 2 stars. This game has the potential to be a 5 star game. The visuals and level design are fantastic. The story is decent and really sucks you in with a great opening intro. Unfortunately, the further I progressed I found myself getting very frustrated.

Issues I had.  (1) There are some items that are just unreachable. Even with the upgrades certain items can't be reached no matter what.

(2) Every time I pause the screen and go back to the game it completely freezes. The entire tittle screen starts shaking and I have to quit the game completely. This has happened just about every time I pause the game.

(3) When turning the dragon to fight enemies there are many occasions the head just stays straight. It's very awkward. It's like im shooting fireballs from the side of his body because his head stays straight ahead. This isn't a huge issue but just feels a bit off.

(4) This is the last and biggest issue I have. I'm currently on level 5 and I am having a hard time switching hands when I press the grip button. The dragon is staying on one side and not responding to my actions. This is a huge problem and might prevent me from progressing through the game. 

If these issues are addressed I feel this game would be a 4 star game at the least. Hopefully the last issue I had could be addressed somehow cause I'd at least like to finish the game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback. Feel free to join our Discord if you have any issues, we're hard at working making improvements.

1) All collectables are reachable eventually with upgrades, and new skills and room scale options provided in the lobby. But we've heard the note enough that we've been making some of the earlier far collectables easier to reach and we're adding two more room scale options.

2) The pause issue has been fixed internally, and the update is in testing. But there are rules about when you are allowed to push an update and we're waiting for when we can do that. But it should be very soon.

3) Can I assume the turning issue bothered you most during one of the Drone Boss encounters? As an indie team this is a tricky problem to solve but I'll look into it. Outside of those two encounters the head direction does a pretty good job of looking where it's needed until the rules are simply impossible to solve for. The body direction faces along the direction of travel to help players understand where they're going easily. But we do our best to make the head turn where it would be natural until you simply can't turn any further without breaking the neck. It's not something that ruins the experience though.

4) This sticky controller does sound like something ruining your experience. I heard one of our testers had this problem and we couldn't reproduce it. I think you have a sticky palm button. It might be possible for us to introduce a dead zone to account for cases like this but with only one person reporting the problem I do worry that it would affect the game feel for everyone else. I'll look into it anyway.

Again thank you for your feedback but do please reach out on Discord or our website if you encounter issues you'd like solved.

Update: We just released an update that addresses most of your concerns. Check out the grab dead zone blue in the options. Increasing that may solve your sticky grab button. We have also done some fixes to the pause menu and reduced the distance you need to reach for some early collectibles. We’ve also added to new room scale options to the lobby and an easy mode. Please reach out on Discord if you still have any issues.

reviews.review_description:
The phoenix gene game is full of action and a story .the graphics are amazing and so are the visuals . here are some reasons why i love the game , you actually get immersed in it because its that good , you battle to save  the future.
 You fight monsters and mutants and drones .you collect the gold flames for upgraded games . this game is great it  even has a dragon and who doesn't love dragons . it has so much more too .

developer_response.body: Thanks dogZface... yes we love Dragons too, this was our unique spin on a Dragon game. Something new. Glad you're enjoying it.

reviews.review_description: The phoenix gene is a great on rail shooter that i realy enjoyed playing and even thougt some parts can be a bit hard and i sometimes had to do them over more times than i whould like to admit i still enjoyed the game a lot and i also think the game has a nice story

developer_response.body: Thank you Zombiemwoan. We're issuing an update very soon that will adjust the difficulty.

reviews.review_title: Game like Xortex/Shooty Skies but better
reviews.review_description: If you play time ago games like Xortex (the lab), Shooty Skies Overdrive or Holo Impact, you know what kind of game is it. But in this case, the narrative it's a strong point and the level disgn are great. When you think, "Okay, I've had enough of this level." The game surprises you with a different kind of level. It's refreshing.

developer_response.body: Excellent, glad you like it. We're doing our best to keep it fresh.

reviews.review_description:
The game is... ok.

Its a great way to get someone who doesnt know the buttons on the controller to master them. Its also a good way to ease them into VR in general as the game is quite heavy on spacial awareness.

Its a great game. However, I'd suggest that if you have a weak stomach... even as a VR pro, it can get quite... sickly.

The game overall though is well thought out and gives the player a nice journey while keeping you on your toes.

4 Stars: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing BritishPerson. I would be very curious where you felt the game was sickly if you would like to contact us on our Discord or anywhere else. We it a lot of care into ensuring that the game never dies the worst things that can cause nausea in VR. But every players weaknesses in this area is different and it’s always possible. Especially if your headset is doing something else in the background that lowers the frame rate. We know the Quest 2 doesn’t handle recording at the same time well and the frame rate can get low. But the Quest 3 handles it fine and still keeps 90fps.

reviews.review_title: VR gymnastics you didn't know you needed
reviews.review_description: Graphically, this game reminds me of holographc games I played 8n the early 90s, except now I'm part of the game, thanks to the innovation of VR!  I needed to clear the room for this one. Maybe it was just the way I wanted to play it. I was rolling all over my lounge room floor - dodging, weaving, shooting, taking out drones from all around me. Being able to swap hands, roll all over the floor, one becomes thankful for passthrough to keep the players safe in their real environment. I didn't have to play like this......but it was sooooo much fun!

developer_response.body: Thank you intjmega... that's how it was intended to be played. Glad you're enjoying it.

reviews.review_description:
My time playing was almost enjoyable. Some fun was had, but the stale gameplay of Phoenix Gene paired with the game's nature to be hard and easy in different game aspects and in different situations at the same time.
The visuals are partially lacking and overall can be compared to what other games offer. Graphically, it's not stunning. In its art style and visual identity, this game is achieving great things. The art style is coherent for the entire game, ehich I can truly appreciate.
But because you mainly have to move your arms in radial motions and the requirements to do such are broad, gameplay can get very stale. This leads to less motivation to play. On the other hand, the story is amazing, and it really holds the game together. Other than that, the intensity of the game is less than what I can call action.
Sadly, and this is my biggest nitpick, the main element, the movement of the dragon, is the only part of motion controls in this game. Any other natural action is attempted to be achieved by a button press. With this wannabe-flatscreen game, few innovations are being made, which leads me to give less than 3 stars.
However, this game can make you motion sick as most of the game is comprised of floating around on what is basically a slow rollercoaster with few stops for bossfights or special scenes.
Graphics: acceptable
Artstyle: truly good
Story: amazing
Gameplay: lacking, incoherent
